prompt: Give me a short pep talk before I start a hard task I have been putting off all morning.
response: You’ve got this. Remember why this matters and that starting is the hardest part—once you take one small step, momentum builds. Set a 10-minute timer, do the single smallest thing that moves it forward, and allow yourself a break after. Be kind to yourself if it’s imperfect; progress beats perfection. Now breathe, start the timer, and go—one step at a time.
